prevalence.source: Epidemiological studies in Brazil + WHO sleep health context
prevalence.trend: increasing with anxiety/depression, shift work, and post-pandemic sleep disruption

burden.mortalityRate: Negligible (indirect via accidents and cardiometabolic risk)
burden.source: IHME/WHO (sleep and mental health burden context)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Insomnia disorder: difficulty initiating/maintaining sleep with daytime impairment ≥3 nights/week for ≥3 months; rule out OSA, restless legs, and substance-induced sleep disturbance

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; CBT-I availability limited, hypnotics often used

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS evaluation with screening for depression/anxiety and sleep apnea; limited access to polysomnography outside capitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: First-line CBT-I and sleep hygiene; short-term hypnotics when indicated; treat comorbid depression/anxiety and pain
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited CBT-I access, overreliance on benzodiazepines in some settings, and low specialist sleep medicine availability
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: APS network can deliver sleep hygiene counseling and manage comorbid mental health conditions
